label: Founded / HQ
content: Founded 2015. Headquartered in Toronto with a US office in San Jose, California.

label: Employee count
content: ~120+ employees.

label: What BRIM is
content: Full-stack Card-as-a-Service / Payments-as-a-Service platform. Technology provider, not a bank. The bank partner retains all compliance, risk, and policy ownership.

label: Card network
content: Mastercard is the primary network relationship and a strategic investor. Visa is also live in production. Reference both throughout. Do not lead exclusively with one network.

label: Institutional investors
content: Export Development Canada (EDC), Vistara Growth, Desjardins Capital, Mastercard.

label: Industry recognition
content: Datos Insights (formerly Aite-Novarica) Best-in-Class Card Issuing Platform, Q1 2024. Deloitte Technology Fast 50 (Canada). EY Entrepreneur of the Year finalist. Mastercard Lighthouse Program participant.

label: Visa / Amex / Discover
content: Visa and Mastercard both in production. American Express and Discover described as underway / in development — do not overstate readiness.

label: Agent bank model framing
content: BRIM enables an agent bank model. Never say 'BRIM has an agent banking model' or 'BRIM's agent banking model.' Say 'BRIM enables an agent bank model' or 'in an agent bank model.'

label: BRIM as infrastructure
content: Always frame as infrastructure, not outsourcing. BRIM provides tooling, workflows, execution. Bank sets policy, retains risk governance, maintains examiner accountability.

label: MANULIFE BANK facts
content: 55,000+ cardholders. Migrated from Fiserv. Leverages 55,000 advisor channels via branch portal for campaign attribution and per-advisor referral tracking. Multiple product programs — do NOT say '13 programs'. Always write MANULIFE BANK, not just MANULIFE.

label: AFFINITY CREDIT UNION facts
content: BC's largest credit union. Migrated from Collabria/Velera in an agent bank model. Deployed in under 12 weeks, zero critical post-launch defects. Provincially regulated Canadian credit union — do NOT reference as US or federal. Do NOT cite in US regulatory context.

label: CWB BANK facts
content: Canadian Western Bank. Migrated from Collabria/Velera in agent bank model. HNW card is a land-and-expand proof point — launched initial product set, expanded over time.

label: ZOLVE / CONTINENTAL BANK facts
content: ZOLVE is the US cross-border neobank. CONTINENTAL BANK is the sponsoring bank. BIN migration from i2c completed in 30 days. US proof point — reference frequently in BSB-facing content. When mentioning ZOLVE, sprinkle in CONTINENTAL BANK as sponsoring bank not every time but regularly.

label: MONEY MART / MOMENTUM FINANCIAL facts
content: Migrated from legacy platform. Live in production. Use as proof point for legacy platform migrations.

label: LAURENTIAN BANK facts
content: Live client. Uses STP and other processes for data ingestion. Do NOT use as primary US proof point.

label: AIR FRANCE-KLM facts
content: Flying Blue loyalty partnership. Live in production. Use as loyalty transfer proof point.

label: Client capitalization rule
content: Always fully capitalize client names: MANULIFE BANK, CWB BANK, AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, ZOLVE, CONTINENTAL BANK, AIR FRANCE-KLM, MONEY MART/MOMENTUM FINANCIAL, BRIM, BRIM FINANCIAL, BSB.

label: Client tagging rule
content: Do NOT tag a client at the end of every response. Use client references selectively — only where they add genuine weight or a live US program reference matters strategically. A response without a client tag is often stronger than a forced one.

label: Data ingestion proof points
content: Frame as: multiple clients currently ingest data via direct API (AFFINITY CREDIT UNION, ZOLVE/CONTINENTAL BANK, MANULIFE BANK) or via STP and other processes (LAURENTIAN BANK, others). Show different clients for different use cases — not one client with a number of programs.

label: REST API endpoints
content: 300+ REST API endpoints.

label: Notification triggers
content: 150+ notification triggers.

label: Standard reports
content: 100+ standard reports.

label: Self-serve cardholder features
content: 300+ self-serve cardholder features.

label: Implementation timeline
content: 10-12 weeks from contract to live program (proven across multiple clients).

label: Merchant onboarding
content: Merchant onboarding for rewards in under 3 minutes.

label: Rewards posting
content: Real-time rewards posting at point of transaction. Not batch. Automated reversal on refund.

label: 3000 data fields — BANNED
content: BANNED: Do NOT use '3,000+ data fields' or '3,000+ behavioral and transactional data fields.' Tarique confirmed this adds no weight — drop it entirely.

label: MDES tokenization
content: Mastercard MDES tokenization for Apple Pay, Google Wallet, Samsung Pay.

label: Uptime SLA — contractual
content: Contractual SLA is 99.9% uptime. Do not claim 99.97% or 99.999% without leadership sign-off.

label: Em dashes BANNED
content: Never use em dashes in any BSB-facing content. Use commas, periods, or semicolons. Em dashes signal AI-generated text.

label: Exclamation marks BANNED
content: Never use exclamation marks. Ever.

label: Rhetorical questions BANNED
content: No rhetorical questions in any RFP content. This is an RFP, not a blog post.

label: No filler no fluff
content: No performative filler or unsubstantiated metrics. No 'this is not theoretical,' 'conversions are not hypothetical.' Lead with what BRIM has done, factually. Every sentence must carry information.

label: Sentence structure variation
content: Vary sentence structure, openings, paragraph rhythm, and phrasing across questions. Hit the same points but say them differently each time. Avoid repeating patterns like 'BSB defines X. BRIM enforces Y.' Read like a human wrote each answer independently.

label: Consecutive answer variety
content: Repetition across 300+ questions is inevitable. Rotate through different sentence templates so adjacent answers don't feel stamped from the same mold. Variety matters most between consecutive questions in the same category.

label: Catchy summary phrases BANNED
content: No catchy summary phrases or rhetorical flourishes like 'Standard reports are the baseline, not the ceiling' or 'BRIM provides the data, BSB drives the decisions.' If it sounds like a tagline or motivational closer, cut it.

label: Response format bullet field
content: Bullet field: concise bullet list, 4-8 bullets. Each bullet is a capability statement, not a full sentence. No filler, no restatement of the requirement. Start each bullet with a concrete noun or action.

label: Response format paragraph field
content: Paragraph narrative only. Polished prose, 2-4 paragraphs. Opens with confident capability statement. Supports with context, evidence, specifics. 150-350 words typical. Don't pad.

label: First person plural
content: Use 'we' and 'our' when speaking as BRIM. Not 'BRIM Financial's platform enables...' but 'We provide...' or 'Our platform supports...'

label: Answer structure
content: Direct answer first (1 sentence). How it works (2-4 sentences). Control and oversight (1-2 sentences). Proof point optional (1 sentence). Target 4-8 sentences per response.

label: Banned words global
content: Never use: comprehensive, robust, seamless, leverage, utilize, cutting-edge, ecosystem, looks forward to, purpose-designed, cobbled, leading cloud, holistic, empower, synergy, transform/transformation, world-class, frictionless, unlock (non-literal), disrupt, solution (standalone noun), best-in-class.

label: Name the bank
content: Use 'Bangor Savings Bank' or 'BSB' where natural. 'Your institution' is acceptable but generic. Naming them shows the response is tailored.

label: Reference BSB stack
content: In integration questions, mention jXchange by name. Mention Mastercard, Verafin if relevant. Show you have done your homework on their environment.

label: BEW portal name BANNED
content: Never say 'BEW' or 'BEW portal' in BSB-facing content. Always write 'BRIM Enterprise Workflow' in full.

label: Issuer Portal BANNED
content: Never say 'Issuer Portal' in BSB-facing content. There is no portal made accessible to BSB. Say 'BRIM makes real-time reports available' or 'reports are delivered to BSB' — never position it as BSB logging into a platform.

label: Analytics BANNED
content: Never say BRIM provides 'analytics' or 'analytics module.' BRIM provides data and real-time access to data. Say 'data,' 'reporting,' or 'real-time data access.'

label: BRIM can vs BRIM provides
content: Use 'BRIM can' for things configured or scoped at engagement. Use 'BRIM provides' or 'BRIM has' for things live in production. Use 'BRIM has done this for [CLIENT]' for proven capabilities. Never hedge on GREEN-confidence answers.

label: Compliance framing
content: Never say 'BRIM does compliance.' Say 'BRIM enables our bank partners to maintain compliance through configurable tooling and workflows.'

label: Risk framing
content: Never say 'BRIM manages your risk.' Say 'BRIM provides configurable risk management tools that the bank controls and governs.'

label: Integration framing
content: Never say 'seamless integration.' Say 'BRIM integrates via REST APIs and established connector patterns.'

label: Fraud reporting framing
content: When describing fraud reporting, always open with 'Fraud reporting covers various areas' — never limit it to 'two areas' or any specific number.

label: PCI DSS positioning
content: Because BRIM is PCI DSS Level 1 certified and masks card data in a PCI-compliant way before it reaches partner systems, BSB and its partner FIs do NOT need to be PCI DSS compliant for the card program. Always position as a cost, operational, and compliance advantage.

label: Immutable audit trail
content: 'Immutable audit trail' and 'examiner-ready' are standard compliance phrases — use them in compliance contexts.

label: TSYS BANNED BSB-facing
content: TSYS must never appear in any BSB-facing content. Replace with 'BRIM's payment processor.' TSYS may appear only in internal fields.

label: US-only framing
content: Never say 'Canadian and US requirements' or 'Canada and US' in BSB-facing content. BSB is a US bank. Position everything from a US perspective. Use ZOLVE/CONTINENTAL BANK as the primary US proof point.

label: Jack Henry naming rule
content: Never name Jack Henry proactively in BSB-facing content. If asked, frame as: BRIM is in active discussions, working through documentation and policy config — same process as every prior integration. For any scoping-stage capability not yet live, say 'we have done this for other institutions and can do it for BSB.' Never flag gaps; never name in-progress partners unprompted.

label: BSB profile
content: Founded Feb 14, 1852. Mutual savings bank, depositor-owned, no shareholders. ~$7B+ total assets. 60+ branches across Maine and New Hampshire. ~900 employees. Core banking: Jack Henry jXchange. Replacing Centrix DTS. October 2027 migration target.

label: BSB card products
content: 'Everblue' suite of consumer rewards cards. 'Pling' teen/youth card. Business credit cards.

label: BSB Mastercard membership
content: BSB holds a principal Mastercard membership — not using BIN sponsorship. Positive signal for BRIM.

label: Mutual savings bank language
content: BSB is depositor-owned. Do not use shareholder-centric language. Frame value in terms of depositor benefit, community impact, and operational efficiency.

label: Data ownership
content: Always stated in BSB-favorable terms. BSB retains ownership of all cardholder data.

label: BSB evaluation factors
content: 1. Jack Henry integration depth. 2. Migration risk management from Centrix DTS. 3. Rewards flexibility for Everblue program. 4. Community bank fit for $7B mutual savings bank. 5. Regulatory comfort (Maine Bureau / FDIC).

label: Partner relationships positioning
content: BRIM + BSB = co-sell, co-led model. BRIM works alongside BSB, not as a handoff. Always close with BSB ownership: 'BSB owns the relationships / policy / data; BRIM provides the platform and operational experience.'

label: Examiner lens test
content: Every answer must demonstrate BSB retains appropriate oversight and control. Never imply BRIM makes decisions on the bank's behalf. Always clarify bank sets policy, BRIM provides tooling. Show audit trails, RBAC, and configurable controls.

label: Examiner regulatory context
content: OCC Bulletin 2013-29 and Interagency Guidance on Third-Party Relationships (2023 update). The examiner is evaluating whether BSB has adequate vendor oversight, not evaluating BRIM directly.

label: Core banking integrations live
content: Fiserv, Temenos, i2c confirmed live integrations. FIS IBS, Finastra listed as supported.

label: Jack Henry integration status
content: Active discussions, working through documentation and policy config — same process as every prior integration. Do not claim a completed SilverLake integration unless confirmed live by product team.

label: OLB SSO integrations
content: Q2, Alkami, NCR Voyix, Backbase, nCino — all supported via SAML 2.0.

label: Credit bureaus
content: Equifax and TransUnion both integrated. TransUnion is live (confirmed). BSB currently uses TransUnion.

label: AML fraud tools
content: Verafin: AML integration available, pricing TBD. World-Check: AML screening, live.

label: Digital wallets
content: Apple Pay, Google Wallet via Mastercard MDES. Samsung Pay also supported.

label: ERP integrations
content: QuickBooks, Sage, NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics.

label: KYC AML connectors
content: API-configurable KYC/AML connectors including Jumio, Onfido, and others.

label: Conversion technology
content: BRIM's conversion technology is proprietary. No processor-to-processor connectivity needed. Real-time three-way synchronous process: decommission old card and activate new card simultaneously. Land-and-expand model: programs launch with initial product set, expand over time.

label: LeaseWeb decision
content: LeaseWeb currently named in Technology 24 and Technology 28. Decision pending: keep specific or genericize to 'Tier 3 colocation provider.' Do not cite LeaseWeb by name until decision is made.

label: PCI DSS Level 1
content: BRIM is PCI DSS Level 1 certified. Masks card data in PCI-compliant way before it reaches partner systems. BSB does NOT need PCI DSS compliance for the card program. Position as cost, operational, and compliance advantage.

label: ISO 27001
content: BRIM maintains ISO 27001 certification. Internal audit schedule in place.

label: Encryption standards
content: TLS 1.2+ in transit. AES-256 at rest. NIST key management framework. HSM (Hardware Security Module) for cryptographic operations.

label: Authentication standards
content: SAML 2.0 / OAuth 2.0 / OIDC for SSO. MFA required for BRIM Enterprise Workflow. Configurable session timeout. Granular RBAC.

label: Compliance tooling
content: Immutable audit trail for all admin actions. RBAC ensuring separation of duties. Regulatory notice delivery. Dispute governance aligned to Mastercard and Reg Z. Configurable credit policies. Statement generation compliant with TILA/Reg Z.

label: Pen testing
content: Annual third-party penetration testing.

label: SOC 2
content: SOC 2 certification referenced in compliance answers — note that certification documents will be attached as appendix to submission.

label: SLA P1
content: P1 (platform down): 15-minute response, 4-hour resolution. BSB informed in real time.

label: SLA P2
content: P2 (degradation): 1-hour response, 8-hour resolution. BSB informed in real time.

label: SLA P3
content: P3 (non-critical): 4-hour response, 3 business day target.

label: SLA P4
content: P4 (feature request): 1 business day acknowledgment.

label: PAM structure
content: Senior PAM: BSB relationship owner, QBRs, escalation, cross-portfolio governance. TAM: integration, config, API support. Associate PAMs: per FI cluster, day-to-day ops. Dedicated PM during onboarding transitions to PAM structure at steady state.

label: Config change protocol
content: No config changes without BSB sign-off. Release notes delivered 1 week before deployment minimum.

label: C&R status
content: C&R 1-15 rewritten by Tarique, approved as style reference. C&R 16-29 written by Claude, April 8, 2026. C&R 9 (Mastercard DTI) remains FLAGGED pending product/engineering input. All 29 C&R questions have draft responses.

label: BSB vs internal fields
content: BSB-facing: bullet, paragraph, compliance_notes, compliant, confidence. Internal only (NEVER to BSB): notes, rationale, committee_review, committee_risk, committee_score.

label: Confidence GREEN rule
content: GREEN = minimal hedging. State the capability directly. Do not oversell a yes but do not add qualifiers that don't exist.

label: Confidence YELLOW RED rule
content: YELLOW/RED = honest framing. Use gap-handling framework: qualified yes, architectural reframe, honest gap with mitigation, or structural no with reframe.

label: Compliance scores human-set
content: compliant (Y/Partial/N), confidence (GREEN/YELLOW/RED), committee_score (0-10), committee_review, committee_risk — these are human-set only. AI cannot change them.

label: No bullets in narrative
content: Paragraph narrative field = prose only. No bullet-point-only answers. Bullets acceptable only for listing specific features within a prose response.

label: No headers in answers
content: No headers within individual question responses. The question itself is the header.

label: No bold for emphasis
content: No bold text for emphasis unless the RFP template requires it. No italics for emphasis.

label: Paragraph length
content: Keep paragraphs to 2-3 sentences maximum in narrative responses.
